title: feat: route gemini-2.5-flash-lite to Vertex global endpoint
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Gemini goes global
gist.blurb: We re-routed Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite to a global endpoint to pool quotas and dodge rate limits. Turns out the whole world is better than just us-central1.
gist.summary: Updated the Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite configuration to use Vertex's global endpoint instead of a single region, effectively pooling quota to prevent throttling.
gist.impact: Text generation requests using Gemini Flash Lite will be significantly more reliable under heavy load.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a traffic controller for global data. A glowing retro globe showing data streams, with the Nomnom creature eating the old '429 Error' logs.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny traffic controller hat, directing glowing data streams around a chunky retro holographic globe. The round tan nomnom creature sits nearby happily eating a stack of red '429 Error' tickets. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
